senses.definition: a type of exercise that is designed to make one's lungs and heart stronger, often performed with music

examples.x: The instructor led a session of high-energy aerobics, including jumping jacks and step exercises.
The fitness program combined aerobics with strength training for a well-rounded exercise routine.
After a few weeks of regular aerobics, she noticed increased stamina and energy levels.
She added aerobics to her weekly exercise regimen to complement her strength training and improve aerobic capacity.
Aerobics routines often combine jumping, stretching, and running in place.

senses.definition: related to athletes or their career

examples.x: The school's athletic program encourages students to participate in a variety of sports.
The athletic community came together to support the injured athlete during his recovery.
The athletic department organized a charity run to raise funds for local youth sports programs.
His athletic performance in the marathon was impressive.

senses.definition: a room in a school, etc. that contains lockers in which people can change their clothes

examples.x: The football players gathered in the locker room to discuss strategy before the big game.
She left her gym bag in the locker room while she worked out on the treadmill.
The coach gave a motivational speech to the team in the locker room during halftime.
Athletes are expected to keep the locker room clean and tidy at all times.
The basketball team celebrated their victory in the locker room after the championship game.

senses.definition: a metal bar with heavy discs hanging at each end, used in bodybuilding

examples.x: He performed squats with a barbell across his shoulders to target his leg and lower body muscles.
She practiced deadlifts with a barbell to build strength in her back and legs.
The trainer guided him on proper technique for using the barbell to avoid injury and improve results.
After weeks of training, he increased the weight on his barbell to continue challenging his muscles.

senses.definition: two heavy discs with a short handle in between, used in bodybuilding

examples.x: He lifted a pair of dumbbells during his workout to build muscle and increase strength.
The gym has a range of dumbbells in different weights to accommodate various fitness levels.
They included dumbbell exercises in their fitness plan to diversify their strength training routine.
After starting with lighter dumbbells, she gradually increased the weight as her strength improved.

senses.definition: a piece of equipment at a gym with two parts that one stands on while holding two bars and pushing each part and bar as an exercise

examples.x: She prefers using the cross trainer at the gym for a low-impact cardio workout.
The cross trainer's adjustable resistance levels allow users to customize their exercise intensity.
He spends 30 minutes each day on the cross trainer to improve his cardiovascular fitness.
The gym recently upgraded its equipment, including adding new cross trainers with advanced features.
The cross trainer's handlebars provide upper body workout benefits in addition to lower body conditioning.

senses.definition: a bar fixed horizontally above the ground for used for various exercises requiring gripping and pulling motions

examples.x: Gymnasts practice their routines on the horizontal bar, showcasing their strength and agility.
The fitness instructor demonstrated pull-ups on the horizontal bar as part of the workout session.
Children enjoy playing on the horizontal bar at the playground, developing their coordination and strength.
The athlete performed a flawless routine on the horizontal bar, impressing the judges with his skill.

senses.definition: a rope with a handle on each end that is swung over a person's head and under their feet as they jump

examples.x: Every morning, Sarah spends ten minutes using her jump rope to start her day with some cardio exercise.
The children took turns using the brightly colored jump rope during recess.
Jump rope competitions require both skill and endurance, showcasing impressive tricks and synchronization.
Mark bought a weighted jump rope to intensify his workout routine.

senses.definition: a thick plastic or rubber material used in particular sports for landing or lying on

examples.x: After the workout, he rolled up the mat and stored it in the designated area for exercise equipment.
The mat’s non-slip surface helped prevent slipping during intense workouts and stretches.
He used a thick exercise mat to cushion his knees while doing push-ups and other floor exercises.
The gym provided mats for users to perform floor exercises and reduce impact on their joints.

senses.definition: a piece of exercise equipment designed to improve different muscles of the body

examples.x: The home gym was equipped with a multigym that included a bench press, leg press, and pull-down machine.
He used the multigym to perform a full-body workout, switching between exercises for different muscle groups.
They purchased a multigym to save space and have access to multiple types of strength training equipment at home.
After using the multigym regularly, he noticed significant improvements in his overall strength and muscle tone.

senses.definition: a piece of gymnastics equipment with two handles on its surface by which gymnasts can perform particular moves on

examples.x: The gymnast performed a flawless routine on the pommel horse, showcasing his strength and agility.
Training on the pommel horse requires a significant amount of upper body strength and coordination.
During the competition, he lost his grip on the pommel horse and had to restart his routine.
The coach adjusted the height of the pommel horse to match the gymnast's preferred settings.
Mastering the pommel horse is considered one of the most challenging aspects of men's gymnastics.

senses.definition: a piece of exercise equipment with a bar and a sliding seat that improves the same muscles engaged in rowing

examples.x: Lisa uses the rowing machine at the gym to improve her cardiovascular fitness and build muscle strength.
The rowing machine provides a low-impact workout that is easy on the joints while still being highly effective.
He set a new personal best time on the rowing machine after weeks of consistent training.
Many athletes include sessions on the rowing machine in their cross-training routines.
The rowing machine's adjustable resistance settings allow users to customize their workouts according to their fitness levels.

senses.definition: a hanging stuffed bag that is often used for practicing punching and striking techniques in boxing and martial arts

examples.x: The boxer spent hours training with the punching bag to perfect his jabs and hooks.
Hitting the punching bag is a great way to release stress and improve upper body strength.
She installed a punching bag in her garage to practice kickboxing at home.
The coach demonstrated the proper technique for striking the punching bag without injuring one's wrists.
The heavy punching bag swayed back and forth with each powerful punch from the athlete.

senses.definition: a piece of exercise equipment that consists of a stretchy cloth attached to a frame on which people can jump for fun or exercise

examples.x: The children spent the afternoon bouncing on the trampoline in the backyard.
Gymnasts use the trampoline to practice their aerial maneuvers and flips.
Safety nets around the trampoline help prevent accidents while jumping.
The trampoline park was filled with people of all ages enjoying the various bouncing activities.
After installing a trampoline in their garden, the family noticed an increase in their outdoor playtime.

senses.definition: a fitness machine with a moving surface that allows people to walk or run in one place for exercise

examples.x: She used the treadmill at the gym for her daily cardio workout, setting a steady pace for 30 minutes.
The treadmill’s adjustable incline helped him simulate hill running and intensify his exercise.
After a long day, he enjoyed walking on the treadmill while watching TV to unwind.
They purchased a treadmill for home use to maintain their fitness routine regardless of the weather.
He started with a slow walk on the treadmill before gradually increasing his speed to a light jog.

senses.definition: to jump up and down over and over again, especially on a stretchy surface

examples.x: The kids couldn't contain their excitement as they began to bounce on the trampoline in the backyard.
During the concert, the crowd started to bounce to the rhythm of the energetic music.
The fitness class participants used mini trampolines to bounce in a high-intensity workout.
As the song reached its peak, the entire dance floor started to bounce with the energetic beats.
During the celebration, people began to bounce in joy, creating a lively atmosphere.

senses.definition: to jump using one leg

examples.x: During the sack race, the children had to hop to the finish line with one leg inside the sack.
To avoid stepping on hot sand, beachgoers would often hop from one shady spot to another.
The playful toddler hopped around the backyard on one leg.

senses.definition: to extend one's body parts or one's entire body to full length

examples.x: Every morning, she begins her day with a series of yoga poses to stretch her body and improve flexibility.
The cat woke up from its nap and proceeded to stretch its legs and arch its back before hopping off the couch.
Before starting the race, the sprinter takes a moment to stretch his muscles and warm up his body.
Sitting at the desk for hours, he takes regular breaks to stand up and stretch his arms and legs to relieve stiffness.

senses.definition: to make something more powerful

examples.x: Regular exercise can strengthen your muscles and improve overall fitness.
Adding reinforcements will strengthen the structure of the building, making it more stable.
The leader worked to strengthen the team's unity through team-building activities.
Studying consistently will strengthen your understanding of the subject.
You are strengthening your knowledge through continuous learning.

senses.definition: to go to a position in which the knees are bent and the back of thighs are touching or very close to one's heels

examples.x: During the workout, the fitness instructor demonstrated how to squat properly.
The baseball catcher squatted behind the plate, ready to receive the pitcher's throw.
As part of their daily exercise routine, the athletes were instructed to squat to improve flexibility and muscle tone.
In the garden, she squatted down to plant the flowers, taking care to nurture each seedling.
During the camping trip, they had to squat by the fire to cook their meals as there were no chairs available.

senses.definition: to produce small drops of liquid on the surface of one's skin

examples.x: After a long run, he began to sweat profusely.
Working under the hot sun, she could feel herself starting to sweat.
Nervous before the presentation, he started to sweat despite the air-conditioned room.
In the sauna, people intentionally sweat to cleanse their skin.
The athletes were sweating heavily after the intense training session.

senses.definition: an exercise for the arms in which one hangs from a bar and tries to pull oneself up until the chin is above the bar

examples.x: He did ten chin-ups in a row as part of his daily workout routine.
She struggled to complete her first chin-up but felt proud once she achieved it.
The fitness instructor demonstrated proper form for performing a chin-up.
The athlete incorporated chin-ups into his upper body strength training program.

senses.definition: an exercise in which one lies face down and tries to raise one's body off the ground by pushing against the floor

examples.x: She performed a series of push-ups to build upper body strength and improve her fitness.
After a few sets of push-ups, he felt his chest and triceps muscles working hard.
The exercise challenge involved doing as many push-ups as possible in a set time frame.
He started with modified push-ups on his knees before progressing to standard push-ups.

senses.definition: an exercise in which a person can strengthen their stomach muscles by constantly changing from lying to sitting position without moving the legs

examples.x: She performed a set of sit-ups to strengthen her abdominal muscles during her workout.
He struggled with sit-ups at first but noticed significant improvement as he practiced regularly.
After completing a series of sit-ups, he could feel the muscles in his stomach working harder.
They included sit-ups in their morning exercise routine to help build core strength.

senses.definition: an exercise that is performed by jumping from a standing position with legs both spread wide and the hands touching overhead and then returning to a position with the feet together and the arms at the sides

examples.x: The warm-up routine began with a set of jumping jacks to get everyone's heart rates up.
She did a series of jumping jacks before starting her workout to loosen up her muscles.
The fitness instructor demonstrated how to perform jumping jacks correctly for maximum benefit.
To increase the intensity of the exercise, he added jumping jacks between other cardio drills.
The children enjoyed doing jumping jacks during their physical education class, making it a fun activity.

senses.definition: to press or rub a part of a person's body, typically with the hands, to make them feel refreshed

examples.x: The spa therapist used aromatic oils to massage the client's back, promoting relaxation.
The physical therapist employed various techniques to massage the injured athlete's leg for rehabilitation.
To relieve stress, she asked her partner to gently massage her shoulders and neck.
After a long flight, he booked a session to have a professional masseur massage his fatigued legs.

senses.definition: the body's ability to recover and rebuild muscle more efficiently after a period of inactivity

examples.x: After a few weeks away from the gym, she found that her muscle memory helped her quickly return to her previous lifting weights.
The coach emphasized that muscle memory could help athletes recover their skills faster than learning from scratch.
The athlete's muscle memory facilitated a swift return to form after a break from training.
His muscle memory allowed him to quickly pick up where he left off in his sport after a long injury recovery.

senses.definition: a person's strong stomach muscles that are easily visible

examples.x: She admired her progress in the gym as her six-pack began to become visible after months of training.
The fitness magazine featured a cover story on how to get a six-pack through diet and exercise.
The personal trainer demonstrated several exercises that help develop a six-pack.

senses.definition: any type of sports that include fighting which are especially originated in the Far East, such as judo, kung fu, etc.

examples.x: He has been studying martial arts since he was a child, starting with karate and later branching out into jiu-jitsu.
Many martial arts emphasize both physical training and mental discipline.
The dojo offers classes in a wide range of martial arts, including taekwondo, kung fu, and judo.
Practicing martial arts has helped her improve her focus and self-confidence.
Martial arts tournaments attract competitors from around the world to showcase their skills and techniques.

senses.definition: to lift heavy weights as a form of exercise or strength training
to exercise in order to get healthier or stronger

examples.x: I work out with a friend to make the exercise routine more enjoyable.
We need to find time to work out together and motivate each other.
Despite a busy schedule, he manages to work out regularly to maintain his fitness levels.
I try to work out at least three times a week for better cardiovascular health.
She worked out for an hour yesterday after work.

senses.definition: a person's speed when walking, moving, or running

examples.x: The runner maintained a steady pace throughout the marathon, ensuring they did not tire out too quickly.
The pace of the project was too slow, causing delays in the overall timeline.
She adjusted her walking pace to match that of her companion during their hike.
They set a brisk pace for their daily walk, aiming to get their heart rates up.

senses.definition: to consume energy by doing physical activity

examples.x: Swimming is a full-body exercise that can help burn off calories efficiently.
To maintain a healthy weight, it's important to consistently burn off excess calories.
Regular physical activity is essential to burn off excess energy and maintain health.
The new fitness program aims to help participants burn off fat effectively.

senses.definition: a room for athletes to change clothes and prepare for their competition or game

examples.x: The gym's dressing room was equipped with lockers and benches, making it convenient for members to change and store their belongings.
The football team gathered in the dressing room to discuss their strategy before the big match.
After the game, the players returned to the dressing room to change out of their uniforms and cool down.
The coach gave a motivational speech to the team in the dressing room before the final quarter of the game.
The dressing room needed a thorough cleaning after the tournament, with towels and uniforms scattered everywhere.

senses.definition: used to describe the comparative skill, strength, or ability of athletes regardless of their size or weight, especially in boxing

examples.x: Pound for pound, she's one of the strongest athletes on the team.
The boxer is considered pound for pound one of the best in the world due to his exceptional technique and power.
The gymnast is pound for pound one of the strongest athletes in the competition, able to perform incredible feats of strength.
Despite his size, he's considered pound for pound one of the toughest fighters in the boxing world.
